name: Certified
description_short: America's Credit Unions offers the Certified Credit Union Board Member (CCUB) designation, a professional development program for credit union board members to deepen leadership knowledge and governance skills.
description_long:
America's Credit Unions is a trade association and professional development organization that serves the credit union industry. The organization provides educational resources, training programs, and industry conferences for credit union leaders and professionals. Their offerings span governance, compliance, regulatory affairs, and professional certification.

The CCUB designation is their flagship board member certification program, designed to help credit union board members understand their fiduciary responsibilities, governance best practices, and strategic leadership. The program incorporates the NAFCU Certified Volunteer Expert (NCVE) curriculum and offers flexible learning options including in-person Board Certification Schools and self-paced eSchool modules. Completion requires finishing required training modules and passing an exam. The designation must be recertified every three years by attending qualifying America's Credit Unions events or completing recertification eSchools.

What distinguishes America's Credit Unions is their direct affiliation with the credit union industry itself—they are not a third-party vendor but rather the membership organization representing the sector. Their educational content is developed with industry expertise and their events create direct networking opportunities with peers and established credit union leaders. Recertification requirements maintain ongoing engagement with the organization and current industry developments.

It's important to note that this is a professional designation program for board governance, not a consumer financial product or service. The CCUB is intended exclusively for credit union board members and focuses on institutional leadership rather than personal finance solutions. Access to the full program and membership benefits requires joining America's Credit Unions as an organizational member.
services: Certified Credit Union Board Member (CCUB) designation program
Certified Credit Union Board Member eSchool (On Demand, self-paced modules)
Credit Union Board Roundtable attendance (qualifying event)
Supervisory Committee & Internal Audit Conference (qualifying event)
Board & Committee Re-Certification eSchool On Demand
Center for Professional Development (CPD) eSchools and Webinars subscription access
pros: Flexible learning formats: choose between in-person Board Certification School or self-paced eSchool (On Demand available August 2026)
Incorporates NAFCU Certified Volunteer Expert (NCVE) curriculum, enhancing credibility and relevance
Direct networking with industry experts, fellow board members, and established credit union leaders
Multiple recertification pathways including five different qualifying conference types and eSchool options
Professional recognition among peers as a trained, forward-thinking board leader
Integrated subscription model (CPD eSchools and Webinars) provides access to additional training resources
Specifically designed for credit union board members by the leading credit union industry organization
cons: Exclusive to credit union board members—not applicable to general consumers or non-board professionals
Requires active membership with America's Credit Unions to access full program and benefits
Three-year recertification requirement demands ongoing time and financial commitment to maintain designation
No pricing information disclosed on website; costs not transparent for prospective participants
Program is B2B (board governance) rather than a consumer finance product, limiting relevance to most users
best_for: Credit union board members seeking formal governance training and professional credibility
Board chairs and board leadership preparing to guide institutional strategy
Credit union staff responsible for board development and continuing education programs
diagnosis: The CCUB designation is exclusively for credit union board members seeking formal governance training and professional recognition within the credit union industry. This is a B2B professional development program, not a consumer financial product, so it is not relevant to individual consumers seeking personal finance solutions, credit repair, or lending services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
